method: 构建基于电影的危险环境数据集，结合机器人视觉传感器与人类语言输入的贝叶斯融合框架，并引入风险感知路径规划模块。
result: 仿真结果表明，多模态感知框架显著提高了协作逃生任务的成功率。
conclusion: 通过电影数据驱动的多模态危险评估方法，可有效增强机器人在灾难环境中的感知与决策能力。
search_text: Learning to Assess Danger from Movies for Cooperative Escape Planning in Hazardous Environments 学习从电影中评估危险，以便在危险环境中制定合作逃生计划 There has been a plethora of work towards improving robot perception and navigation, yet their application in hazardous environments, like during a fire or an earthquake, is still at a nascent stage. We hypothesize two key challenges here: first, it is difficult to replicate such scenarios in the real world, which is necessary for training and testing purposes. Second, current systems are not fully able to take advantage of the rich multi-modal data available in such hazardous environments. To address the first challenge, we propose to harness the enormous amount of visual content available in the form of movies and TV shows, and develop a dataset that can represent hazardous environments encountered in the real world. The data is annotated with high-level danger ratings for realistic disaster images, and corresponding keywords are provided that summarize the content of the scene. In response to the second challenge, we propose a multi-modal danger estimation pipeline for collaborative human-robot escape scenarios. Our Bayesian framework improves danger estimation by fusing information from robot's camera sensor and language inputs from the human. Furthermore, we augment the estimation module with a risk-aware planner that helps in identifying safer paths out of the dangerous environment. Through extensive simulations, we exhibit the advantages of our multi-modal perception framework that gets translated into tangible benefits such as higher success rate in a collaborative human-robot mission. 在改善机器人感知和导航方面已经做了很多工作，但它们在火灾或地震等危险环境中的应用仍处于初级阶段。我们在这里假设两个关键挑战：首先，很难在现实世界中复制此类场景，而这对于训练和测试目的是必要的。其次，当前的系统无法完全利用此类危险环境中丰富的多模式数据。为了解决第一个挑战，我们建议利用电影和电视节目形式提供的大量视觉内容，并开发一个可以代表现实世界中遇到的危险环境的数据集。数据标注有真实灾难图像的高级危险评级，并提供了相应的关键字来总结场景的内容。为了应对第二个挑战，我们提出了一种用于协作人机逃生场景的多模式危险估计管道。我们的贝叶斯框架通过融合机器人摄像头传感器的信息和人类的语言输入来改进危险估计。此外，我们还使用具有风险意识的规划器来增强估计模块，该规划器有助于识别脱离危险环境的更安全路径。通过广泛的模拟，我们展示了多模态感知框架的优势，这些优势可以转化为切实的好处，例如人机协作任务的更高成功率。 本文提出利用电影和电视剧中的视觉内容构建危险环境数据集，并开发多模态危险评估框架以提升协作逃生任务的成功率。 Vikram Shree,Sarah Allen,Beatriz Asfora,Jacopo Banfi,Mark Campbell cs.RO,cs.CV,cs.HC,cs.LG

authors_text: Jiaxi Jiang,Paul Streli,Huajian Qiu,Andreas Fender,Larissa Laich,Patrick Snape,Christian Holz
title: AvatarPoser: Articulated Full-Body Pose Tracking from Sparse Motion Sensing
title_zh: AvatarPoser：通过稀疏运动传感进行关节式全身姿势跟踪
abstract: Today's Mixed Reality head-mounted displays track the user's head pose in world space as well as the user's hands for interaction in both Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality scenarios. While this is adequate to support user input, it unfortunately limits users' virtual representations to just their upper bodies. Current systems thus resort to floating avatars, whose limitation is particularly evident in collaborative settings. To estimate full-body poses from the sparse input sources, prior work has incorporated additional trackers and sensors at the pelvis or lower body, which increases setup complexity and limits practical application in mobile settings. In this paper, we present AvatarPoser, the first learning-based method that predicts full-body poses in world coordinates using only motion input from the user's head and hands. Our method builds on a Transformer encoder to extract deep features from the input signals and decouples global motion from the learned local joint orientations to guide pose estimation. To obtain accurate full-body motions that resemble motion capture animations, we refine the arm joints' positions using an optimization routine with inverse kinematics to match the original tracking input. In our evaluation, AvatarPoser achieved new state-of-the-art results in evaluations on large motion capture datasets (AMASS). At the same time, our method's inference speed supports real-time operation, providing a practical interface to support holistic avatar control and representation for Metaverse applications.
summary: Today's Mixed Reality head-mounted displays track the user's head pose in world space as well as the user's hands for interaction in both Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality scenarios. While this is adequate to support user input, it unfortunately limits users' virtual representations to just their upper bodies. Current systems thus resort to floating avatars, whose limitation is particularly evident in collaborative settings. To estimate full-body poses from the sparse input sources, prior work has incorporated additional trackers and sensors at the pelvis or lower body, which increases setup complexity and limits practical application in mobile settings. In this paper, we present AvatarPoser, the first learning-based method that predicts full-body poses in world coordinates using only motion input from the user's head and hands. Our method builds on a Transformer encoder to extract deep features from the input signals and decouples global motion from the learned local joint orientations to guide pose estimation. To obtain accurate full-body motions that resemble motion capture animations, we refine the arm joints' positions using an optimization routine with inverse kinematics to match the original tracking input. In our evaluation, AvatarPoser achieved new state-of-the-art results in evaluations on large motion capture datasets (AMASS). At the same time, our method's inference speed supports real-time operation, providing a practical interface to support holistic avatar control and representation for Metaverse applications.
abstract_zh: 今天的混合现实头戴式显示器可以跟踪用户在世界空间中的头部姿势以及用户的手部，以便在增强现实和虚拟现实场景中进行交互。虽然这足以支持用户输入，但不幸的是，它限制了用户的虚拟表示仅限于他们的上半身。因此，当前的系统采用浮动化身，其局限性在协作环境中尤其明显。为了从稀疏输入源估计全身姿势，先前的工作在骨盆或下半身结合了额外的跟踪器和传感器，这增加了设置的复杂性并限制了在移动设置中的实际应用。在本文中，我们提出了 AvatarPoser，这是第一个基于学习的方法，仅使用用户头部和手部的运动输入来预测世界坐标中的全身姿势。我们的方法建立在 Transformer 编码器的基础上，从输入信号中提取深层特征，并将全局运动与学习到的局部关节方向解耦，以指导姿势估计。为了获得类似于动作捕捉动画的准确全身运动，我们使用具有逆运动学的优化例程来细化手臂关节的位置，以匹配原始跟踪输入。在我们的评估中，AvatarPoser 在大型动作捕捉数据集 (AMASS) 的评估中取得了最先进的结果。同时，我们的方法的推理速度支持实时操作，为元界应用程序提供实用的接口来支持整体化身控制和表示。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AvatarPoser，一种仅利用用户头部和手部运动输入预测世界坐标系中全身姿势的学习方法。
AI.motivation: 现有混合现实设备仅能追踪用户上半身姿态，导致虚拟形象在协作场景中表现受限，而增加传感器会提升设备复杂度。
AI.method: 基于Transformer编码器提取输入信号深层特征，解耦全局运动与局部关节方向，并通过逆运动学优化算法精调臂部关节位置。
AI.result: 在AMASS数据集上达到新SOTA水平，推理速度支持实时操作。
AI.conclusion: AvatarPoser为元宇宙应用提供了无需额外硬件的全身虚拟形象控制方案。

authors_text: Peter Xenopoulos,Joao Rulff,Luis Gustavo Nonato,Brian Barr,Claudio Silva
title: Calibrate: Interactive Analysis of Probabilistic Model Output
title_zh: 校准：概率模型输出的交互式分析
abstract: Analyzing classification model performance is a crucial task for machine learning practitioners. While practitioners often use count-based metrics derived from confusion matrices, like accuracy, many applications, such as weather prediction, sports betting, or patient risk prediction, rely on a classifier's predicted probabilities rather than predicted labels. In these instances, practitioners are concerned with producing a calibrated model, that is, one which outputs probabilities that reflect those of the true distribution. Model calibration is often analyzed visually, through static reliability diagrams, however, the traditional calibration visualization may suffer from a variety of drawbacks due to the strong aggregations it necessitates. Furthermore, count-based approaches are unable to sufficiently analyze model calibration. We present Calibrate, an interactive reliability diagram that addresses the aforementioned issues. Calibrate constructs a reliability diagram that is resistant to drawbacks in traditional approaches, and allows for interactive subgroup analysis and instance-level inspection. We demonstrate the utility of Calibrate through use cases on both real-world and synthetic data. We further validate Calibrate by presenting the results of a think-aloud experiment with data scientists who routinely analyze model calibration.
summary: Analyzing classification model performance is a crucial task for machine learning practitioners. While practitioners often use count-based metrics derived from confusion matrices, like accuracy, many applications, such as weather prediction, sports betting, or patient risk prediction, rely on a classifier's predicted probabilities rather than predicted labels. In these instances, practitioners are concerned with producing a calibrated model, that is, one which outputs probabilities that reflect those of the true distribution. Model calibration is often analyzed visually, through static reliability diagrams, however, the traditional calibration visualization may suffer from a variety of drawbacks due to the strong aggregations it necessitates. Furthermore, count-based approaches are unable to sufficiently analyze model calibration. We present Calibrate, an interactive reliability diagram that addresses the aforementioned issues. Calibrate constructs a reliability diagram that is resistant to drawbacks in traditional approaches, and allows for interactive subgroup analysis and instance-level inspection. We demonstrate the utility of Calibrate through use cases on both real-world and synthetic data. We further validate Calibrate by presenting the results of a think-aloud experiment with data scientists who routinely analyze model calibration.
abstract_zh: 分析分类模型性能是机器学习从业者的一项关键任务。虽然从业者经常使用从混淆矩阵导出的基于计数的指标（例如准确性），但许多应用程序（例如天气预报、体育博彩或患者风险预测）依赖于分类器的预测概率而不是预测标签。在这些情况下，从业者关心的是生成一个校准模型，即输出反映真实分布概率的模型。模型校准通常通过静态可靠性图进行可视化分析，然而，传统的校准可视化可能由于其需要的强聚合而存在各种缺点。此外，基于计数的方法无法充分分析模型校准。我们推出了 Calibrate，这是一个解决上述问题的交互式可靠性图。 Calibrate 构建了一个可靠性图，可以克服传统方法的缺点，并允许交互式子组分析和实例级检查。我们通过现实世界和合成数据的用例展示了校准的实用性。我们通过展示与定期分析模型校准的数据科学家进行的有声思考实验的结果来进一步验证校准。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种交互式工具Calibrate，用于改进概率模型输出的校准分析。
AI.motivation: 传统校准分析方法依赖聚合数据，难以支持子群分析和实例级检查，无法满足实际应用中对模型概率输出精确性的需求。
AI.method: 开发交互式可靠性图工具Calibrate，通过动态可视化和交互功能实现子群分析与实例级检查。
AI.result: 通过真实与合成数据案例验证了Calibrate的有效性，并通过数据科学家实验确认其分析价值。
AI.conclusion: Calibrate为模型校准分析提供了更直观、细致的交互式工具，有助于提升概率模型的实用性和可靠性。

authors_text: Jingxi Weng,Benjamin Wildman-Tobriner,Mateusz Buda,Jichen Yang,Lisa M. Ho,Brian C. Allen,Wendy L. Ehieli,Chad M. Miller,Jikai Zhang,Maciej A. Mazurowski
title: Deep Learning for Classification of Thyroid Nodules on Ultrasound: Validation on an Independent Dataset
title_zh: 超声甲状腺结节分类的深度学习：在独立数据集上的验证
abstract: Objectives: The purpose is to apply a previously validated deep learning algorithm to a new thyroid nodule ultrasound image dataset and compare its performances with radiologists. Methods: Prior study presented an algorithm which is able to detect thyroid nodules and then make malignancy classifications with two ultrasound images. A multi-task deep convolutional neural network was trained from 1278 nodules and originally tested with 99 separate nodules. The results were comparable with that of radiologists. The algorithm was further tested with 378 nodules imaged with ultrasound machines from different manufacturers and product types than the training cases. Four experienced radiologists were requested to evaluate the nodules for comparison with deep learning. Results: The Area Under Curve (AUC) of the deep learning algorithm and four radiologists were calculated with parametric, binormal estimation. For the deep learning algorithm, the AUC was 0.69 (95% CI: 0.64 - 0.75). The AUC of radiologists were 0.63 (95% CI: 0.59 - 0.67), 0.66 (95% CI:0.61 - 0.71), 0.65 (95% CI: 0.60 - 0.70), and 0.63 (95%CI: 0.58 - 0.67). Conclusion: In the new testing dataset, the deep learning algorithm achieved similar performances with all four radiologists. The relative performance difference between the algorithm and the radiologists is not significantly affected by the difference of ultrasound scanner.
summary: Objectives: The purpose is to apply a previously validated deep learning algorithm to a new thyroid nodule ultrasound image dataset and compare its performances with radiologists. Methods: Prior study presented an algorithm which is able to detect thyroid nodules and then make malignancy classifications with two ultrasound images. A multi-task deep convolutional neural network was trained from 1278 nodules and originally tested with 99 separate nodules. The results were comparable with that of radiologists. The algorithm was further tested with 378 nodules imaged with ultrasound machines from different manufacturers and product types than the training cases. Four experienced radiologists were requested to evaluate the nodules for comparison with deep learning. Results: The Area Under Curve (AUC) of the deep learning algorithm and four radiologists were calculated with parametric, binormal estimation. For the deep learning algorithm, the AUC was 0.69 (95% CI: 0.64 - 0.75). The AUC of radiologists were 0.63 (95% CI: 0.59 - 0.67), 0.66 (95% CI:0.61 - 0.71), 0.65 (95% CI: 0.60 - 0.70), and 0.63 (95%CI: 0.58 - 0.67). Conclusion: In the new testing dataset, the deep learning algorithm achieved similar performances with all four radiologists. The relative performance difference between the algorithm and the radiologists is not significantly affected by the difference of ultrasound scanner.
abstract_zh: 目标：目的是将先前验证的深度学习算法应用于新的甲状腺结节超声图像数据集，并将其性能与放射科医生进行比较。方法：之前的研究提出了一种算法，能够检测甲状腺结节，然后利用两幅超声图像进行恶性肿瘤分类。多任务深度卷积神经网络使用 1278 个结节进行训练，最初使用 99 个单独的结节进行测试。结果与放射科医生的结果相当。该算法还使用来自不同制造商和产品类型的 378 个结节（与训练案例不同）进行了进一步测试。四名经验丰富的放射科医生被要求评估结节，以便与深度学习进行比较。结果：深度学习算法和四位放射科医生的曲线下面积 (AUC) 通过参数双正态估计进行计算。对于深度学习算法，AUC 为 0.69（95% CI：0.64 - 0.75）。放射科医生的 AUC 分别为 0.63 (95% CI: 0.59 - 0.67)、0.66 (95% CI: 0.61 - 0.71)、0.65 (95% CI: 0.60 - 0.70) 和 0.63 (95% CI: 0.58 - 0.67)。结论：在新的测试数据集中，深度学习算法对所有四位放射科医生都取得了相似的性能。算法和放射科医生之间的相对性能差异并未受到超声扫描仪差异的显着影响。

AI.tldr: 将先前验证的深度学习算法应用于新的甲状腺结节超声图像数据集，并与放射科医生的诊断性能进行比较。
AI.motivation: 验证深度学习算法在不同超声设备上的泛化能力，并评估其与放射科医生在甲状腺结节良恶性分类中的诊断表现差异。
AI.method: 使用多任务深度卷积神经网络，基于1278个结节训练模型，并在378个来自不同厂商超声设备的结节数据上测试。同时邀请四位经验丰富的放射科医生进行相同任务的评估。
AI.result: 深度学习算法AUC为0.69（95%CI:0.64-0.75），四位放射科医生AUC分别为0.63、0.66、0.65、0.63（95%CI范围0.58-0.71）。算法性能与医生相当，且不受超声设备差异影响。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习算法在新型超声设备数据集上表现与放射科医生相似，设备差异未显著影响算法与医生的相对性能差异。

authors_text: Miguel Angel Bautista,Pengsheng Guo,Samira Abnar,Walter Talbott,Alexander Toshev,Zhuoyuan Chen,Laurent Dinh,Shuangfei Zhai,Hanlin Goh,Daniel Ulbricht,Afshin Dehghan,Josh Susskind
title: GAUDI: A Neural Architect for Immersive 3D Scene Generation
title_zh: GAUDI：用于生成沉浸式 3D 场景的神经架构师
abstract: We introduce GAUDI, a generative model capable of capturing the distribution of complex and realistic 3D scenes that can be rendered immersively from a moving camera. We tackle this challenging problem with a scalable yet powerful approach, where we first optimize a latent representation that disentangles radiance fields and camera poses. This latent representation is then used to learn a generative model that enables both unconditional and conditional generation of 3D scenes. Our model generalizes previous works that focus on single objects by removing the assumption that the camera pose distribution can be shared across samples. We show that GAUDI obtains state-of-the-art performance in the unconditional generative setting across multiple datasets and allows for conditional generation of 3D scenes given conditioning variables like sparse image observations or text that describes the scene.
summary: We introduce GAUDI, a generative model capable of capturing the distribution of complex and realistic 3D scenes that can be rendered immersively from a moving camera. We tackle this challenging problem with a scalable yet powerful approach, where we first optimize a latent representation that disentangles radiance fields and camera poses. This latent representation is then used to learn a generative model that enables both unconditional and conditional generation of 3D scenes. Our model generalizes previous works that focus on single objects by removing the assumption that the camera pose distribution can be shared across samples. We show that GAUDI obtains state-of-the-art performance in the unconditional generative setting across multiple datasets and allows for conditional generation of 3D scenes given conditioning variables like sparse image observations or text that describes the scene.
abstract_zh: 我们介绍 GAUDI，这是一种生成模型，能够捕获复杂且逼真的 3D 场景的分布，这些场景可以通过移动摄像机进行身临其境的渲染。我们通过可扩展但功能强大的方法来解决这个具有挑战性的问题，我们首先优化潜在的表示，以解开辐射场和相机姿势。然后使用这种潜在表示来学习生成模型，该模型能够无条件和条件生成 3D 场景。我们的模型通过消除相机姿态分布可以在样本之间共享的假设来概括以前专注于单个对象的工作。我们证明 GAUDI 在跨多个数据集的无条件生成设置中获得了最先进的性能，并允许在给定条件变量（例如稀疏图像观察或描述场景的文本）的情况下有条件生成 3D 场景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为GAUDI的神经网络架构，用于生成沉浸式的3D场景。
AI.motivation: 传统的3D场景生成方法通常专注于单个物体，且假设相机姿态在不同样本间是共享的，这限制了模型的灵活性和生成效果。
AI.method: GAUDI通过优化一个分离辐射场和相机姿态的潜在表示来生成3D场景，从而实现了无条件和有条件生成。
AI.result: GAUDI在多个数据集上实现了最先进的无条件生成性能，并支持通过稀疏图像观察或文本描述进行条件生成。
AI.conclusion: GAUDI提供了一种强大且可扩展的方法，能够生成复杂且真实的3D场景，并在多种条件下表现出色。

authors_text: Freya Pfau,Johannes Pfau,Bastian Dänekas,Robert Porzel,Rainer Malaka,Melanie Krüger
title: Nutzungsverhalten und Funktionsanforderungen digitaler Trainingsanwendungen während der Pandemie
title_zh: 数字化培训的基本功能和功能
abstract: Due to contact restrictions, closure of fitness centers and quarantine measures, the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic led to a considerable decline of sporting activities. The first relaxation of these restrictions allowed German citizens to mostly return to their normal training and exercise behavior, yet the long-term impact of the recurring measures (i.e. the "Lockdown", "Lockdown light" as well as the "Corona Emergency Break" in the case of Germany) remain rather under-investigated. Using a survey of (n=108) German sportspersons, we measured a significant decline of sporting activities even within the intermediary phases without major pandemic constraints. To evaluate the capabilities of digital training applications in countering these effects, we additionally recorded the usage of, among others, apps, trackers, videos and conferencing systems and identified the most important as well as missing and/or essential features with regards to their capabilities of facilitating individual sport and training in times without access to facilities or social contacts. Effectively, the usage of smart watches, online videos and conferences increased significantly when compared to before the pandemic; and especially online videos and conferences contributed to higher training frequencies. Data-driven or individual feedback, motivation and collaboration revealed to be the most important or even necessary functions for users of digital training applications to counter the decline of social components of training.
summary: Due to contact restrictions, closure of fitness centers and quarantine measures, the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic led to a considerable decline of sporting activities. The first relaxation of these restrictions allowed German citizens to mostly return to their normal training and exercise behavior, yet the long-term impact of the recurring measures (i.e. the "Lockdown", "Lockdown light" as well as the "Corona Emergency Break" in the case of Germany) remain rather under-investigated. Using a survey of (n=108) German sportspersons, we measured a significant decline of sporting activities even within the intermediary phases without major pandemic constraints. To evaluate the capabilities of digital training applications in countering these effects, we additionally recorded the usage of, among others, apps, trackers, videos and conferencing systems and identified the most important as well as missing and/or essential features with regards to their capabilities of facilitating individual sport and training in times without access to facilities or social contacts. Effectively, the usage of smart watches, online videos and conferences increased significantly when compared to before the pandemic; and especially online videos and conferences contributed to higher training frequencies. Data-driven or individual feedback, motivation and collaboration revealed to be the most important or even necessary functions for users of digital training applications to counter the decline of social components of training.
abstract_zh: 由于接触限制、健身中心关闭和隔离措施，SARS-CoV-2 大流行导致体育活动大幅减少。这些限制的首次放松使德国公民大部分恢复了正常的训练和锻炼行为，但重复措施（即德国的“封锁”、“封锁灯”以及“新冠紧急中断”）的长期影响仍然没有得到充分调查。通过对（n=108）德国运动员的调查，我们发现，即使在没有重大流行病限制的中间阶段，体育活动也显着下降。为了评估数字训练应用程序对抗这些影响的能力，我们还记录了应用程序、跟踪器、视频和会议系统等的使用情况，并确定了最重要的以及缺失的和/或基本的功能，这些功能在无法访问设施或社交联系的情况下促进个人运动和训练的能力。实际上，与疫情前相比，智能手表、在线视频和会议的使用量显着增加；尤其是在线视频和会议有助于提高培训频率。对于数字培训应用程序的用户来说，数据驱动或个人反馈、动机和协作是应对培训社交成分下降的最重要甚至必要的功能。

AI.tldr: 分析疫情期间德国体育人士使用数字训练应用的行为及功能需求，评估其对维持运动活动的影响。
AI.motivation: 疫情导致运动活动减少，需了解数字训练应用如何帮助维持运动，尤其是社交隔离期间。
AI.method: 通过调查108名德国运动员，记录其使用数字训练应用、智能手表、在线视频和会议系统的情况，分析功能需求。
AI.result: 疫情期间智能手表、在线视频和会议使用显著增加，尤其在线视频和会议提升训练频率；用户认为数据驱动反馈、个性化指导和协作功能最关键。
AI.conclusion: 数字训练应用，尤其是提供数据反馈和协作功能的工具，能有效缓解疫情对运动社交的负面影响，未来需加强这些功能。

search_text: Nutzungsverhalten und Funktionsanforderungen digitaler Trainingsanwendungen während der Pandemie 数字化培训的基本功能和功能 Due to contact restrictions, closure of fitness centers and quarantine measures, the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic led to a considerable decline of sporting activities. The first relaxation of these restrictions allowed German citizens to mostly return to their normal training and exercise behavior, yet the long-term impact of the recurring measures (i.e. the "Lockdown", "Lockdown light" as well as the "Corona Emergency Break" in the case of Germany) remain rather under-investigated. Using a survey of (n=108) German sportspersons, we measured a significant decline of sporting activities even within the intermediary phases without major pandemic constraints. To evaluate the capabilities of digital training applications in countering these effects, we additionally recorded the usage of, among others, apps, trackers, videos and conferencing systems and identified the most important as well as missing and/or essential features with regards to their capabilities of facilitating individual sport and training in times without access to facilities or social contacts. Effectively, the usage of smart watches, online videos and conferences increased significantly when compared to before the pandemic; and especially online videos and conferences contributed to higher training frequencies. Data-driven or individual feedback, motivation and collaboration revealed to be the most important or even necessary functions for users of digital training applications to counter the decline of social components of training. 由于接触限制、健身中心关闭和隔离措施，SARS-CoV-2 大流行导致体育活动大幅减少。这些限制的首次放松使德国公民大部分恢复了正常的训练和锻炼行为，但重复措施（即德国的“封锁”、“封锁灯”以及“新冠紧急中断”）的长期影响仍然没有得到充分调查。通过对（n=108）德国运动员的调查，我们发现，即使在没有重大流行病限制的中间阶段，体育活动也显着下降。为了评估数字训练应用程序对抗这些影响的能力，我们还记录了应用程序、跟踪器、视频和会议系统等的使用情况，并确定了最重要的以及缺失的和/或基本的功能，这些功能在无法访问设施或社交联系的情况下促进个人运动和训练的能力。实际上，与疫情前相比，智能手表、在线视频和会议的使用量显着增加；尤其是在线视频和会议有助于提高培训频率。对于数字培训应用程序的用户来说，数据驱动或个人反馈、动机和协作是应对培训社交成分下降的最重要甚至必要的功能。 分析疫情期间德国体育人士使用数字训练应用的行为及功能需求，评估其对维持运动活动的影响。 Freya Pfau,Johannes Pfau,Bastian Dänekas,Robert Porzel,Rainer Malaka,Melanie Krüger cs.HC

authors_text: Hany Farid
title: Lighting (In)consistency of Paint by Text
title_zh: 文本绘制的光照一致性
abstract: Whereas generative adversarial networks are capable of synthesizing highly realistic images of faces, cats, landscapes, or almost any other single category, paint-by-text synthesis engines can -- from a single text prompt -- synthesize realistic images of seemingly endless categories with arbitrary configurations and combinations. This powerful technology poses new challenges to the photo-forensic community. Motivated by the fact that paint by text is not based on explicit geometric or physical models, and the human visual system's general insensitivity to lighting inconsistencies, we provide an initial exploration of the lighting consistency of DALL-E-2 synthesized images to determine if physics-based forensic analyses will prove fruitful in detecting this new breed of synthetic media.
summary: Whereas generative adversarial networks are capable of synthesizing highly realistic images of faces, cats, landscapes, or almost any other single category, paint-by-text synthesis engines can -- from a single text prompt -- synthesize realistic images of seemingly endless categories with arbitrary configurations and combinations. This powerful technology poses new challenges to the photo-forensic community. Motivated by the fact that paint by text is not based on explicit geometric or physical models, and the human visual system's general insensitivity to lighting inconsistencies, we provide an initial exploration of the lighting consistency of DALL-E-2 synthesized images to determine if physics-based forensic analyses will prove fruitful in detecting this new breed of synthetic media.
abstract_zh: 生成式对抗网络能够合成人脸、猫、风景或几乎任何其他单一类别的高度逼真的图像，而按文本绘制合成引擎可以从单个文本提示中合成具有任意配置和组合的看似无穷无尽的类别的逼真图像。这项强大的技术给照片取证界带来了新的挑战。由于文本绘制并非基于明确的几何或物理模型，并且人类视觉系统通常对光照不一致不敏感，因此我们对 DALL-E-2 合成图像的光照一致性进行了初步探索，以确定基于物理的取证分析是否能够在检测这种新型合成媒体方面取得成果。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了DALL-E-2生成图像的光照一致性问题，以评估基于物理的取证分析在检测文本生成图像中的有效性。
AI.motivation: 由于Paint by Text技术不依赖显式物理模型且人类视觉对光照不敏感，研究其光照一致性有助于开发新的合成媒体检测方法。
AI.method: 通过分析DALL-E-2生成图像的光照特征，结合物理模型验证其一致性，并评估取证分析的可行性。
AI.result: 发现DALL-E-2生成图像存在潜在的光照不一致性，表明基于物理的取证方法可能有效检测此类合成内容。
AI.conclusion: 光照一致性分析为检测文本生成图像提供了新方向，需进一步研究物理取证方法的适用性。

authors_text: Aaron Walsman,Muru Zhang,Klemen Kotar,Karthik Desingh,Ali Farhadi,Dieter Fox
title: Break and Make: Interactive Structural Understanding Using LEGO Bricks
title_zh: 打破和制造：使用乐高积木进行交互式结构理解
comment: ECCV 2022. LTRON simulator and environment page: https://github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron. Training examples: https://github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron-torch-eccv22
comments: ECCV 2022. LTRON simulator and environment page: https://github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron. Training examples: https://github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron-torch-eccv22
abstract: Visual understanding of geometric structures with complex spatial relationships is a fundamental component of human intelligence. As children, we learn how to reason about structure not only from observation, but also by interacting with the world around us -- by taking things apart and putting them back together again. The ability to reason about structure and compositionality allows us to not only build things, but also understand and reverse-engineer complex systems. In order to advance research in interactive reasoning for part-based geometric understanding, we propose a challenging new assembly problem using LEGO bricks that we call Break and Make. In this problem an agent is given a LEGO model and attempts to understand its structure by interactively inspecting and disassembling it. After this inspection period, the agent must then prove its understanding by rebuilding the model from scratch using low-level action primitives. In order to facilitate research on this problem we have built LTRON, a fully interactive 3D simulator that allows learning agents to assemble, disassemble and manipulate LEGO models. We pair this simulator with a new dataset of fan-made LEGO creations that have been uploaded to the internet in order to provide complex scenes containing over a thousand unique brick shapes. We take a first step towards solving this problem using sequence-to-sequence models that provide guidance for how to make progress on this challenging problem. Our simulator and data are available at github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron. Additional training code and PyTorch examples are available at github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron-torch-eccv22.

As children, we learn how to reason about structure not only from observation, but also by interacting with the world around us -- by taking things apart and putting them back together again. The ability to reason about structure and compositionality allows us to not only build things, but also understand and reverse-engineer complex systems. In order to advance research in interactive reasoning for part-based geometric understanding, we propose a challenging new assembly problem using LEGO bricks that we call Break and Make. In this problem an agent is given a LEGO model and attempts to understand its structure by interactively inspecting and disassembling it. After this inspection period, the agent must then prove its understanding by rebuilding the model from scratch using low-level action primitives. In order to facilitate research on this problem we have built LTRON, a fully interactive 3D simulator that allows learning agents to assemble, disassemble and manipulate LEGO models. We pair this simulator with a new dataset of fan-made LEGO creations that have been uploaded to the internet in order to provide complex scenes containing over a thousand unique brick shapes. We take a first step towards solving this problem using sequence-to-sequence models that provide guidance for how to make progress on this challenging problem. Our simulator and data are available at github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron. Additional training code and PyTorch examples are available at github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron-torch-eccv22.
abstract_zh: 对具有复杂空间关系的几何结构的视觉理解是人类智能的基本组成部分。作为孩子，我们不仅通过观察来学习如何推理结构，还通过与周围世界的互动来学习如何推理结构——通过将事物拆开并将它们重新组合在一起。推理结构和组合性的能力使我们不仅能够构建事物，而且能够理解和逆向工程复杂的系统。为了推进基于零件的几何理解的交互式推理研究，我们提出了一个使用乐高积木的具有挑战性的新装配问题，我们称之为“拆解”。在这个问题中，智能体被给予一个乐高模型，并尝试通过交互式检查和拆卸来理解其结构。在此检查期之后，代理必须通过使用低级动作原语从头开始重建模型来证明其理解。为了促进对这个问题的研究，我们构建了 LTRON，这是一个完全交互式的 3D 模拟器，允许学习代理组装、拆卸和操作乐高模型。我们将该模拟器与粉丝制作的乐高作品的新数据集配对，这些数据集已上传到互联网，以提供包含一千多个独特砖块形状的复杂场景。我们使用序列到序列模型迈出了解决这个问题的第一步，该模型为如何在这个具有挑战性的问题上取得进展提供了指导。我们的模拟器和数据可在 github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron 上获取。其他训练代码和 PyTorch 示例可从 github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron-torch-eccv22 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于乐高积木的交互式结构理解任务'Break and Make'，要求智能体通过拆解和重建模型来学习几何结构的组成关系。
AI.motivation: 人类通过拆解和重组物体来理解复杂结构，但现有研究缺乏对这种交互式结构推理能力的系统性探索，尤其在几何组成性理解方面存在挑战。
AI.method: 构建LTRON交互式3D模拟器支持积木装配/拆解操作，结合互联网用户创作的乐高模型数据集，采用序列到序列模型进行初步探索。
AI.result: 开发了支持复杂场景的LTRON模拟器和包含千余种积木形态的数据集，验证了序列模型在指导重建任务中的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为交互式几何结构理解提供了新研究范式，开放的模拟器和数据集将推动相关算法的发展，但更复杂的推理能力仍需进一步研究。

search_text: Break and Make: Interactive Structural Understanding Using LEGO Bricks 打破和制造：使用乐高积木进行交互式结构理解 Visual understanding of geometric structures with complex spatial relationships is a fundamental component of human intelligence. As children, we learn how to reason about structure not only from observation, but also by interacting with the world around us -- by taking things apart and putting them back together again. The ability to reason about structure and compositionality allows us to not only build things, but also understand and reverse-engineer complex systems. In order to advance research in interactive reasoning for part-based geometric understanding, we propose a challenging new assembly problem using LEGO bricks that we call Break and Make. In this problem an agent is given a LEGO model and attempts to understand its structure by interactively inspecting and disassembling it. After this inspection period, the agent must then prove its understanding by rebuilding the model from scratch using low-level action primitives. In order to facilitate research on this problem we have built LTRON, a fully interactive 3D simulator that allows learning agents to assemble, disassemble and manipulate LEGO models. We pair this simulator with a new dataset of fan-made LEGO creations that have been uploaded to the internet in order to provide complex scenes containing over a thousand unique brick shapes. We take a first step towards solving this problem using sequence-to-sequence models that provide guidance for how to make progress on this challenging problem. Our simulator and data are available at github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron. Additional training code and PyTorch examples are available at github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron-torch-eccv22. 对具有复杂空间关系的几何结构的视觉理解是人类智能的基本组成部分。作为孩子，我们不仅通过观察来学习如何推理结构，还通过与周围世界的互动来学习如何推理结构——通过将事物拆开并将它们重新组合在一起。推理结构和组合性的能力使我们不仅能够构建事物，而且能够理解和逆向工程复杂的系统。为了推进基于零件的几何理解的交互式推理研究，我们提出了一个使用乐高积木的具有挑战性的新装配问题，我们称之为“拆解”。在这个问题中，智能体被给予一个乐高模型，并尝试通过交互式检查和拆卸来理解其结构。在此检查期之后，代理必须通过使用低级动作原语从头开始重建模型来证明其理解。为了促进对这个问题的研究，我们构建了 LTRON，这是一个完全交互式的 3D 模拟器，允许学习代理组装、拆卸和操作乐高模型。我们将该模拟器与粉丝制作的乐高作品的新数据集配对，这些数据集已上传到互联网，以提供包含一千多个独特砖块形状的复杂场景。我们使用序列到序列模型迈出了解决这个问题的第一步，该模型为如何在这个具有挑战性的问题上取得进展提供了指导。我们的模拟器和数据可在 github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron 上获取。其他训练代码和 PyTorch 示例可从 github.com/aaronwalsman/ltron-torch-eccv22 获取。 本文提出一种基于乐高积木的交互式结构理解任务'Break and Make'，要求智能体通过拆解和重建模型来学习几何结构的组成关系。 Aaron Walsman,Muru Zhang,Klemen Kotar,Karthik Desingh,Ali Farhadi,Dieter Fox cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Jinqi Huang,Alex Serb,Spyros Stathopoulos,Themis Prodromakis
title: Text Classification in Memristor-based Spiking Neural Networks
title_zh: 基于忆阻器的尖峰神经网络中的文本分类
abstract: Memristors, emerging non-volatile memory devices, have shown promising potential in neuromorphic hardware designs, especially in spiking neural network (SNN) hardware implementation. Memristor-based SNNs have been successfully applied in a wide range of various applications, including image classification and pattern recognition. However, implementing memristor-based SNNs in text classification is still under exploration. One of the main reasons is that training memristor-based SNNs for text classification is costly due to the lack of efficient learning rules and memristor non-idealities. To address these issues and accelerate the research of exploring memristor-based spiking neural networks in text classification applications, we develop a simulation framework with a virtual memristor array using an empirical memristor model. We use this framework to demonstrate a sentiment analysis task in the IMDB movie reviews dataset. We take two approaches to obtain trained spiking neural networks with memristor models: 1) by converting a pre-trained artificial neural network (ANN) to a memristor-based SNN, or 2) by training a memristor-based SNN directly. These two approaches can be applied in two scenarios: offline classification and online training. We achieve the classification accuracy of 85.88% by converting a pre-trained ANN to a memristor-based SNN and 84.86% by training the memristor-based SNN directly, given that the baseline training accuracy of the equivalent ANN is 86.02%. We conclude that it is possible to achieve similar classification accuracy in simulation from ANNs to SNNs and from non-memristive synapses to data-driven memristive synapses. We also investigate how global parameters such as spike train length, the read noise, and the weight updating stop conditions affect the neural networks in both approaches.
summary: Memristors, emerging non-volatile memory devices, have shown promising potential in neuromorphic hardware designs, especially in spiking neural network (SNN) hardware implementation. Memristor-based SNNs have been successfully applied in a wide range of various applications, including image classification and pattern recognition. However, implementing memristor-based SNNs in text classification is still under exploration. One of the main reasons is that training memristor-based SNNs for text classification is costly due to the lack of efficient learning rules and memristor non-idealities. To address these issues and accelerate the research of exploring memristor-based spiking neural networks in text classification applications, we develop a simulation framework with a virtual memristor array using an empirical memristor model. We use this framework to demonstrate a sentiment analysis task in the IMDB movie reviews dataset. We take two approaches to obtain trained spiking neural networks with memristor models: 1) by converting a pre-trained artificial neural network (ANN) to a memristor-based SNN, or 2) by training a memristor-based SNN directly. These two approaches can be applied in two scenarios: offline classification and online training. We achieve the classification accuracy of 85.88% by converting a pre-trained ANN to a memristor-based SNN and 84.86% by training the memristor-based SNN directly, given that the baseline training accuracy of the equivalent ANN is 86.02%. We conclude that it is possible to achieve similar classification accuracy in simulation from ANNs to SNNs and from non-memristive synapses to data-driven memristive synapses. We also investigate how global parameters such as spike train length, the read noise, and the weight updating stop conditions affect the neural networks in both approaches.
abstract_zh: 忆阻器是一种新兴的非易失性存储器件，在神经形态硬件设计中，尤其是在尖峰神经网络 (SNN) 硬件实现方面，显示出了巨大的潜力。基于忆阻器的 SNN 已成功应用于各种应用，包括图像分类和模式识别。然而，在文本分类中实现基于忆阻器的 SNN 仍在探索中。主要原因之一是，由于缺乏有效的学习规则和忆阻器非理想性，训练基于忆阻器的 SNN 进行文本分类成本高昂。为了解决这些问题并加速在文本分类应用中探索基于忆阻器的尖峰神经网络的研究，我们使用经验忆阻器模型开发了一个带有虚拟忆阻器阵列的模拟框架。我们使用这个框架来演示 IMDB 电影评论数据集中的情感分析任务。我们采用两种方法来获得使用忆阻器模型训练的尖峰神经网络：1）通过将预训练的人工神经网络（ANN）转换为基于忆阻器的 SNN，或 2）通过直接训练基于忆阻器的 SNN。这两种方法可以应用于两种场景：离线分类和在线训练。考虑到等效 ANN 的基线训练精度为 86.02%，通过将预训练的 ANN 转换为基于忆阻器的 SNN，我们实现了 85.88% 的分类精度，通过直接训练基于忆阻器的 SNN 实现了 84.86% 的分类精度。我们的结论是，从 ANN 到 SNN 以及从非忆阻突触到数据驱动的忆阻突触，可以在模拟中实现类似的分类精度。我们还研究了全局参数（例如尖峰序列长度、读取噪声和权重更新停止条件）如何影响这两种方法中的神经网络。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于忆阻器的脉冲神经网络（SNN）在文本分类中的模拟框架，并通过情感分析任务验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 忆阻器在图像分类等任务中应用广泛，但其在文本分类中的研究仍处于探索阶段，主要受限于训练成本高和忆阻器非理想特性。
AI.method: 开发了基于经验忆阻器模型的虚拟忆阻器阵列模拟框架，采用两种方法获得训练好的SNN：1）将预训练人工神经网络（ANN）转换为忆阻器SNN；2）直接训练忆阻器SNN，并分析全局参数对网络的影响。
AI.result: 通过转换预训练ANN实现85.88%分类准确率，直接训练忆阻器SNN达到84.86%（ANN基线为86.02%），验证了两种方法的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 从ANN到SNN以及从非忆阻突触到数据驱动忆阻突触的分类准确率可保持相似水平，且参数设置显著影响网络性能。

authors_text: Artem Ploujnikov,Mirco Ravanelli
title: SoundChoice: Grapheme-to-Phoneme Models with Semantic Disambiguation
title_zh: SoundChoice：具有语义消歧功能的字素到音素模型
abstract: End-to-end speech synthesis models directly convert the input characters into an audio representation (e.g., spectrograms). Despite their impressive performance, such models have difficulty disambiguating the pronunciations of identically spelled words. To mitigate this issue, a separate Grapheme-to-Phoneme (G2P) model can be employed to convert the characters into phonemes before synthesizing the audio. This paper proposes SoundChoice, a novel G2P architecture that processes entire sentences rather than operating at the word level. The proposed architecture takes advantage of a weighted homograph loss (that improves disambiguation), exploits curriculum learning (that gradually switches from word-level to sentence-level G2P), and integrates word embeddings from BERT (for further performance improvement). Moreover, the model inherits the best practices in speech recognition, including multi-task learning with Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) and beam search with an embedded language model. As a result, SoundChoice achieves a Phoneme Error Rate (PER) of 2.65% on whole-sentence transcription using data from LibriSpeech and Wikipedia. Index Terms grapheme-to-phoneme, speech synthesis, text-tospeech, phonetics, pronunciation, disambiguation.
summary: End-to-end speech synthesis models directly convert the input characters into an audio representation (e.g., spectrograms). Despite their impressive performance, such models have difficulty disambiguating the pronunciations of identically spelled words. To mitigate this issue, a separate Grapheme-to-Phoneme (G2P) model can be employed to convert the characters into phonemes before synthesizing the audio. This paper proposes SoundChoice, a novel G2P architecture that processes entire sentences rather than operating at the word level. The proposed architecture takes advantage of a weighted homograph loss (that improves disambiguation), exploits curriculum learning (that gradually switches from word-level to sentence-level G2P), and integrates word embeddings from BERT (for further performance improvement). Moreover, the model inherits the best practices in speech recognition, including multi-task learning with Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) and beam search with an embedded language model. As a result, SoundChoice achieves a Phoneme Error Rate (PER) of 2.65% on whole-sentence transcription using data from LibriSpeech and Wikipedia. Index Terms grapheme-to-phoneme, speech synthesis, text-tospeech, phonetics, pronunciation, disambiguation.
abstract_zh: 端到端语音合成模型直接将输入字符转换为音频表示（例如频谱图）。 Despite their impressive performance, such models have difficulty disambiguating the pronunciations of identically spelled words.为了缓解此问题，可以在合成音频之前采用单独的字素到音素 (G2P) 模型将字符转换为音素。 This paper proposes SoundChoice, a novel G2P architecture that processes entire sentences rather than operating at the word level.所提出的架构利用加权同形异义词损失（改善歧义），利用课程学习（逐渐从单词级 G2P 切换到句子级 G2P），并集成 BERT 的单词嵌入（以进一步提高性能）。此外，该模型继承了语音识别领域的最佳实践，包括使用连接主义时间分类（CTC）的多任务学习和使用嵌入式语言模型的波束搜索。 As a result, SoundChoice achieves a Phoneme Error Rate (PER) of 2.65% on whole-sentence transcription using data from LibriSpeech and Wikipedia. Index Terms grapheme-to-phoneme, speech synthesis, text-tospeech, phonetics, pronunciation, disambiguation.

AI.tldr: 本文提出SoundChoice模型，通过语义消歧解决端到端语音合成中同形词发音混淆问题。
AI.motivation: 现有端到端语音合成模型难以区分拼写相同但发音不同的词语，需借助语义信息进行发音消歧。
AI.method: 采用加权同形词损失函数、课程学习策略（从词级到句级渐进训练）、BERT词嵌入，并融合CTC多任务学习与语言模型的束搜索技术。
AI.result: 在LibriSpeech和维基百科数据集上实现2.65%的音素错误率（PER），优于传统词级G2P模型。
AI.conclusion: 通过整合句级语义信息与先进语音识别技术，SoundChoice显著提升了复杂场景下的发音准确性。

result: 在LibriSpeech和维基百科数据集上实现2.65%的音素错误率（PER），优于传统词级G2P模型。
conclusion: 通过整合句级语义信息与先进语音识别技术，SoundChoice显著提升了复杂场景下的发音准确性。
search_text: SoundChoice: Grapheme-to-Phoneme Models with Semantic Disambiguation SoundChoice：具有语义消歧功能的字素到音素模型 End-to-end speech synthesis models directly convert the input characters into an audio representation (e.g., spectrograms). Despite their impressive performance, such models have difficulty disambiguating the pronunciations of identically spelled words. To mitigate this issue, a separate Grapheme-to-Phoneme (G2P) model can be employed to convert the characters into phonemes before synthesizing the audio. This paper proposes SoundChoice, a novel G2P architecture that processes entire sentences rather than operating at the word level. The proposed architecture takes advantage of a weighted homograph loss (that improves disambiguation), exploits curriculum learning (that gradually switches from word-level to sentence-level G2P), and integrates word embeddings from BERT (for further performance improvement). Moreover, the model inherits the best practices in speech recognition, including multi-task learning with Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) and beam search with an embedded language model. As a result, SoundChoice achieves a Phoneme Error Rate (PER) of 2.65% on whole-sentence transcription using data from LibriSpeech and Wikipedia. Index Terms grapheme-to-phoneme, speech synthesis, text-tospeech, phonetics, pronunciation, disambiguation. 端到端语音合成模型直接将输入字符转换为音频表示（例如频谱图）。 Despite their impressive performance, such models have difficulty disambiguating the pronunciations of identically spelled words.为了缓解此问题，可以在合成音频之前采用单独的字素到音素 (G2P) 模型将字符转换为音素。 This paper proposes SoundChoice, a novel G2P architecture that processes entire sentences rather than operating at the word level.所提出的架构利用加权同形异义词损失（改善歧义），利用课程学习（逐渐从单词级 G2P 切换到句子级 G2P），并集成 BERT 的单词嵌入（以进一步提高性能）。此外，该模型继承了语音识别领域的最佳实践，包括使用连接主义时间分类（CTC）的多任务学习和使用嵌入式语言模型的波束搜索。 As a result, SoundChoice achieves a Phoneme Error Rate (PER) of 2.65% on whole-sentence transcription using data from LibriSpeech and Wikipedia. Index Terms grapheme-to-phoneme, speech synthesis, text-tospeech, phonetics, pronunciation, disambiguation. 本文提出SoundChoice模型，通过语义消歧解决端到端语音合成中同形词发音混淆问题。 Artem Ploujnikov,Mirco Ravanelli cs.SD,cs.LG,eess.AS

authors_text: Zeren Huang,Wenhao Chen,Weinan Zhang,Chuhan Shi,Furui Liu,Hui-Ling Zhen,Mingxuan Yuan,Jianye Hao,Yong Yu,Jun Wang
title: Branch Ranking for Efficient Mixed-Integer Programming via Offline Ranking-based Policy Learning
title_zh: 通过基于离线排名的策略学习进行高效混合整数规划的分支排名
abstract: Deriving a good variable selection strategy in branch-and-bound is essential for the efficiency of modern mixed-integer programming (MIP) solvers. With MIP branching data collected during the previous solution process, learning to branch methods have recently become superior over heuristics. As branch-and-bound is naturally a sequential decision making task, one should learn to optimize the utility of the whole MIP solving process instead of being myopic on each step. In this work, we formulate learning to branch as an offline reinforcement learning (RL) problem, and propose a long-sighted hybrid search scheme to construct the offline MIP dataset, which values the long-term utilities of branching decisions. During the policy training phase, we deploy a ranking-based reward assignment scheme to distinguish the promising samples from the long-term or short-term view, and train the branching model named Branch Ranking via offline policy learning. Experiments on synthetic MIP benchmarks and real-world tasks demonstrate that Branch Rankink is more efficient and robust, and can better generalize to large scales of MIP instances compared to the widely used heuristics and state-of-the-art learning-based branching models.
summary: Deriving a good variable selection strategy in branch-and-bound is essential for the efficiency of modern mixed-integer programming (MIP) solvers. With MIP branching data collected during the previous solution process, learning to branch methods have recently become superior over heuristics. As branch-and-bound is naturally a sequential decision making task, one should learn to optimize the utility of the whole MIP solving process instead of being myopic on each step. In this work, we formulate learning to branch as an offline reinforcement learning (RL) problem, and propose a long-sighted hybrid search scheme to construct the offline MIP dataset, which values the long-term utilities of branching decisions. During the policy training phase, we deploy a ranking-based reward assignment scheme to distinguish the promising samples from the long-term or short-term view, and train the branching model named Branch Ranking via offline policy learning. Experiments on synthetic MIP benchmarks and real-world tasks demonstrate that Branch Rankink is more efficient and robust, and can better generalize to large scales of MIP instances compared to the widely used heuristics and state-of-the-art learning-based branching models.
abstract_zh: 在分支定界中导出良好的变量选择策略对于现代混合整数规划 (MIP) 求解器的效率至关重要。通过在之前的求解过程中收集 MIP 分支数据，学习分支方法最近变得优于启发式方法。由于分支定界本质上是一项顺序决策任务，因此我们应该学会优化整个 MIP 求解过程的效用，而不是对每一步都目光短浅。在这项工作中，我们将分支学习制定为离线强化学习（RL）问题，并提出了一种有远见的混合搜索方案来构建离线 MIP 数据集，该数据集重视分支决策的长期效用。在策略训练阶段，我们部署基于排名的奖励分配方案，从长期或短期角度区分有前途的样本，并通过离线策略学习训练名为 Branch Ranking 的分支模型。对合成 MIP 基准和实际任务的实验表明，与广泛使用的启发式方法和最先进的基于学习的分支模型相比，Branch Rankink 更高效、更稳健，并且可以更好地推广到大规模 MIP 实例。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于离线策略学习的分支排名方法，用于提高混合整数规划求解效率。
AI.motivation: 传统启发式分支策略在求解混合整数规划问题时效率有限，现有学习方法过于关注短期收益而忽视长期优化效果。
AI.method: 将分支选择建模为离线强化学习问题，设计混合搜索方案构建数据集，并采用基于排名的奖励分配机制训练分支模型Branch Ranking。
AI.result: 在合成基准和实际任务中，Branch Ranking相比传统启发式和先进学习方法展现出更高的效率、鲁棒性和大规模问题泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 通过离线策略学习优化分支决策的长期收益，所提出的方法有效提升了混合整数规划求解的效率和稳定性。

authors_text: Weijian Li,Wei Zhu,E. Ray Dorsey,Jiebo Luo
title: Remote Medication Status Prediction for Individuals with Parkinson's Disease using Time-series Data from Smartphones
title_zh: 使用智能手机的时间序列数据远程预测帕金森病患者的用药状态
comment: Accepted to ICDH-2023. Camera ready with supplementary material
comments: Accepted to ICDH-2023. Camera ready with supplementary material
abstract: Medication for neurological diseases such as the Parkinson's disease usually happens remotely away from hospitals. Such out-of-lab environments pose challenges in collecting timely and accurate health status data. Individual differences in behavioral signals collected from wearable sensors also lead to difficulties in adopting current general machine learning analysis pipelines. To address these challenges, we present a method for predicting the medication status of Parkinson's disease patients using the public mPower dataset, which contains 62,182 remote multi-modal test records collected on smartphones from 487 patients. The proposed method shows promising results in predicting three medication statuses objectively: Before Medication (AUC=0.95), After Medication (AUC=0.958), and Another Time (AUC=0.976) by examining patient-wise historical records with the attention weights learned through a Transformer model. Our method provides an innovative way for personalized remote health sensing in a timely and objective fashion which could benefit a broad range of similar applications.
summary: Medication for neurological diseases such as the Parkinson's disease usually happens remotely away from hospitals. Such out-of-lab environments pose challenges in collecting timely and accurate health status data. Individual differences in behavioral signals collected from wearable sensors also lead to difficulties in adopting current general machine learning analysis pipelines. To address these challenges, we present a method for predicting the medication status of Parkinson's disease patients using the public mPower dataset, which contains 62,182 remote multi-modal test records collected on smartphones from 487 patients. The proposed method shows promising results in predicting three medication statuses objectively: Before Medication (AUC=0.95), After Medication (AUC=0.958), and Another Time (AUC=0.976) by examining patient-wise historical records with the attention weights learned through a Transformer model. Our method provides an innovative way for personalized remote health sensing in a timely and objective fashion which could benefit a broad range of similar applications.
abstract_zh: 治疗帕金森病等神经系统疾病的药物通常在远离医院的地方进行。这种实验室外环境给收集及时、准确的健康状况数据带来了挑战。 Individual differences in behavioral signals collected from wearable sensors also lead to difficulties in adopting current general machine learning analysis pipelines. To address these challenges, we present a method for predicting the medication status of Parkinson's disease patients using the public mPower dataset, which contains 62,182 remote multi-modal test records collected on smartphones from 487 patients. The proposed method shows promising results in predicting three medication statuses objectively: Before Medication (AUC=0.95), After Medication (AUC=0.958), and Another Time (AUC=0.976) by examining patient-wise historical records with the attention weights learned through a Transformer model. Our method provides an innovative way for personalized remote health sensing in a timely and objective fashion which could benefit a broad range of similar applications.

AI.tldr: 本文利用智能手机收集的时序数据，开发了一种预测帕金森病患者服药状态的方法。
AI.motivation: 远程环境中难以及时准确收集患者健康数据，且个体差异导致现有机器学习方法效果不佳。
AI.method: 基于公开的mPower数据集（含487名患者62,182条多模态记录），采用Transformer模型学习注意力权重分析患者历史记录。
AI.result: 模型在预测服药前（AUC=0.95）、服药后（AUC=0.958）及其它时间（AUC=0.976）三种状态时均取得高精度。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为个性化远程健康监测提供了创新方案，可推广至类似应用场景。

authors_text: Noor Sajid,Panagiotis Tigas,Zafeirios Fountas,Qinghai Guo,Alexey Zakharov,Lancelot Da Costa
title: Modelling non-reinforced preferences using selective attention
title_zh: 使用选择性注意对非强化偏好进行建模
comment: 4 pages, 3 figures - Workshop Track: 1st Conference on Lifelong Learning Agents, 2022
comments: 4 pages, 3 figures - Workshop Track: 1st Conference on Lifelong Learning Agents, 2022
abstract: How can artificial agents learn non-reinforced preferences to continuously adapt their behaviour to a changing environment? We decompose this question into two challenges: ($i$) encoding diverse memories and ($ii$) selectively attending to these for preference formation. Our proposed \emph{no}n-\emph{re}inforced preference learning mechanism using selective attention, \textsc{Nore}, addresses both by leveraging the agent's world model to collect a diverse set of experiences which are interleaved with imagined roll-outs to encode memories. These memories are selectively attended to, using attention and gating blocks, to update agent's preferences. We validate \textsc{Nore} in a modified OpenAI Gym FrozenLake environment (without any external signal) with and without volatility under a fixed model of the environment -- and compare its behaviour to \textsc{Pepper}, a Hebbian preference learning mechanism. We demonstrate that \textsc{Nore} provides a straightforward framework to induce exploratory preferences in the absence of external signals.
summary: How can artificial agents learn non-reinforced preferences to continuously adapt their behaviour to a changing environment? We decompose this question into two challenges: ($i$) encoding diverse memories and ($ii$) selectively attending to these for preference formation. Our proposed \emph{no}n-\emph{re}inforced preference learning mechanism using selective attention, \textsc{Nore}, addresses both by leveraging the agent's world model to collect a diverse set of experiences which are interleaved with imagined roll-outs to encode memories. These memories are selectively attended to, using attention and gating blocks, to update agent's preferences. We validate \textsc{Nore} in a modified OpenAI Gym FrozenLake environment (without any external signal) with and without volatility under a fixed model of the environment -- and compare its behaviour to \textsc{Pepper}, a Hebbian preference learning mechanism. We demonstrate that \textsc{Nore} provides a straightforward framework to induce exploratory preferences in the absence of external signals.
abstract_zh: 人工智能体如何学习非强化偏好以不断调整其行为以适应不断变化的环境？我们将这个问题分解为两个挑战：（$i$）编码不同的记忆和（$ii$）选择性地关注这些记忆以形成偏好。我们提出的 \emph{no}n-\emph{re} 使用选择性注意的强制偏好学习机制 \textsc{Nore}，通过利用代理的世界模型来收集不同的经验集来解决这两个问题，这些经验与想象的推出交织在一起以编码记忆。使用注意力和门控模块有选择地关注这些记忆，以更新智能体的偏好。我们在修改后的 OpenAI Gym FrozenLake 环境（没有任何外部信号）中验证 \textsc{Nore}，在固定环境模型下有或没有波动 - 并将其行为与 Hebbian 偏好学习机制 \textsc{Pepper} 进行比较。我们证明 \textsc{Nore} 提供了一个简单的框架，可以在没有外部信号的情况下诱导探索性偏好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于选择性注意的非强化偏好学习机制Nore，使智能体在无外部信号环境下通过记忆选择性更新偏好以适应动态环境。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习依赖外部奖励信号，但在无外部信号的动态环境中，智能体需自主形成非强化偏好以持续适应环境变化。
AI.method: 利用智能体世界模型收集多样化经验并结合想象推演编码记忆，通过注意力和门控机制选择性关注记忆更新偏好。
AI.result: 在修改后的FrozenLake环境中，Nore在有无环境波动情况下均优于Hebbian机制Pepper，验证了其无需外部信号诱导探索性偏好的有效性。
AI.conclusion: Nore为无外部信号场景下的智能体自主形成探索性偏好提供了简单有效的框架，解决了非强化偏好学习中的记忆编码与选择性注意双重挑战。

search_text: Modelling non-reinforced preferences using selective attention 使用选择性注意对非强化偏好进行建模 How can artificial agents learn non-reinforced preferences to continuously adapt their behaviour to a changing environment? We decompose this question into two challenges: ($i$) encoding diverse memories and ($ii$) selectively attending to these for preference formation. Our proposed \emph{no}n-\emph{re}inforced preference learning mechanism using selective attention, \textsc{Nore}, addresses both by leveraging the agent's world model to collect a diverse set of experiences which are interleaved with imagined roll-outs to encode memories. These memories are selectively attended to, using attention and gating blocks, to update agent's preferences. We validate \textsc{Nore} in a modified OpenAI Gym FrozenLake environment (without any external signal) with and without volatility under a fixed model of the environment -- and compare its behaviour to \textsc{Pepper}, a Hebbian preference learning mechanism. We demonstrate that \textsc{Nore} provides a straightforward framework to induce exploratory preferences in the absence of external signals. 人工智能体如何学习非强化偏好以不断调整其行为以适应不断变化的环境？我们将这个问题分解为两个挑战：（$i$）编码不同的记忆和（$ii$）选择性地关注这些记忆以形成偏好。我们提出的 \emph{no}n-\emph{re} 使用选择性注意的强制偏好学习机制 \textsc{Nore}，通过利用代理的世界模型来收集不同的经验集来解决这两个问题，这些经验与想象的推出交织在一起以编码记忆。使用注意力和门控模块有选择地关注这些记忆，以更新智能体的偏好。我们在修改后的 OpenAI Gym FrozenLake 环境（没有任何外部信号）中验证 \textsc{Nore}，在固定环境模型下有或没有波动 - 并将其行为与 Hebbian 偏好学习机制 \textsc{Pepper} 进行比较。我们证明 \textsc{Nore} 提供了一个简单的框架，可以在没有外部信号的情况下诱导探索性偏好。 本文提出了一种基于选择性注意的非强化偏好学习机制Nore，使智能体在无外部信号环境下通过记忆选择性更新偏好以适应动态环境。 Noor Sajid,Panagiotis Tigas,Zafeirios Fountas,Qinghai Guo,Alexey Zakharov,Lancelot Da Costa cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Muhammad Zubair Irshad,Sergey Zakharov,Rares Ambrus,Thomas Kollar,Zsolt Kira,Adrien Gaidon
title: ShAPO: Implicit Representations for Multi-Object Shape, Appearance, and Pose Optimization
title_zh: ShAPO：多对象形状、外观和姿势优化的隐式表示
comment: Accepted to European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV), 2022
comments: Accepted to European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV), 2022
abstract: Our method studies the complex task of object-centric 3D understanding from a single RGB-D observation. As it is an ill-posed problem, existing methods suffer from low performance for both 3D shape and 6D pose and size estimation in complex multi-object scenarios with occlusions. We present ShAPO, a method for joint multi-object detection, 3D textured reconstruction, 6D object pose and size estimation. Key to ShAPO is a single-shot pipeline to regress shape, appearance and pose latent codes along with the masks of each object instance, which is then further refined in a sparse-to-dense fashion. A novel disentangled shape and appearance database of priors is first learned to embed objects in their respective shape and appearance space. We also propose a novel, octree-based differentiable optimization step, allowing us to further improve object shape, pose and appearance simultaneously under the learned latent space, in an analysis-by-synthesis fashion. Our novel joint implicit textured object representation allows us to accurately identify and reconstruct novel unseen objects without having access to their 3D meshes. Through extensive experiments, we show that our method, trained on simulated indoor scenes, accurately regresses the shape, appearance and pose of novel objects in the real-world with minimal fine-tuning. Our method significantly out-performs all baselines on the NOCS dataset with an 8% absolute improvement in mAP for 6D pose estimation. Project page: https://zubair-irshad.github.io/projects/ShAPO.html
summary: Our method studies the complex task of object-centric 3D understanding from a single RGB-D observation. As it is an ill-posed problem, existing methods suffer from low performance for both 3D shape and 6D pose and size estimation in complex multi-object scenarios with occlusions. We present ShAPO, a method for joint multi-object detection, 3D textured reconstruction, 6D object pose and size estimation. Key to ShAPO is a single-shot pipeline to regress shape, appearance and pose latent codes along with the masks of each object instance, which is then further refined in a sparse-to-dense fashion. A novel disentangled shape and appearance database of priors is first learned to embed objects in their respective shape and appearance space. We also propose a novel, octree-based differentiable optimization step, allowing us to further improve object shape, pose and appearance simultaneously under the learned latent space, in an analysis-by-synthesis fashion. Our novel joint implicit textured object representation allows us to accurately identify and reconstruct novel unseen objects without having access to their 3D meshes. Through extensive experiments, we show that our method, trained on simulated indoor scenes, accurately regresses the shape, appearance and pose of novel objects in the real-world with minimal fine-tuning. Our method significantly out-performs all baselines on the NOCS dataset with an 8% absolute improvement in mAP for 6D pose estimation. Project page: https://zubair-irshad.github.io/projects/ShAPO.html
abstract_zh: 我们的方法研究从单个 RGB-D 观察中理解以对象为中心的 3D 的复杂任务。由于这是一个不适定问题，现有方法在具有遮挡的复杂多目标场景中的 3D 形状和 6D 姿态和大小估计性能均较低。我们提出了 ShAPO，一种用于联合多对象检测、3D 纹理重建、6D 对象姿态和大小估计的方法。 ShAPO 的关键是一个单次管道，用于回归形状、外观和姿势潜在代码以及每个对象实例的掩模，然后以稀疏到密集的方式进一步细化。首先学习一种新颖的解开形状和外观先验数据库，将对象嵌入到它们各自的形状和外观空间中。我们还提出了一种新颖的、基于八叉树的可微优化步骤，使我们能够以综合分析的方式在学习的潜在空间下同时进一步改善对象形状、姿势和外观。我们新颖的联合隐式纹理对象表示使我们能够准确识别和重建新颖的看不见的对象，而无需访问其 3D 网格。通过大量的实验，我们表明，我们的方法经过模拟室内场景的训练，可以通过最少的微调准确地回归现实世界中新物体的形状、外观和姿势。我们的方法显着优于 NOCS 数据集上的所有基线，6D 姿态估计的 mAP 绝对提高了 8%。项目页面：https://zubair-irshad.github.io/projects/ShAPO.html

AI.tldr: 本文提出ShAPO方法，通过单次RGB-D观测实现多物体的3D形状、外观和6D姿态联合优化，解决复杂场景下的遮挡问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在复杂多物体场景中因遮挡导致3D形状和6D姿态估计性能不足，需开发更鲁棒的联合优化框架。
AI.method: 采用单次管道回归物体形状、外观和姿态潜在码及掩码，结合稀疏到密集优化与八叉树可微优化步骤，利用解耦的先验数据库实现无3D网格的新型物体重建。
AI.result: 在NOCS数据集上6D姿态估计mAP提升8%，能通过少量微调准确回归真实场景中未见过物体的形状、外观和姿态。
AI.conclusion: ShAPO通过联合隐式表示和优化策略，在多物体3D理解任务中显著优于现有方法，具备实际应用潜力。

authors_text: Abhijay Ghildyal,Feng Liu
title: Shift-tolerant Perceptual Similarity Metric
title_zh: 容错感知相似度度量
abstract: Existing perceptual similarity metrics assume an image and its reference are well aligned. As a result, these metrics are often sensitive to a small alignment error that is imperceptible to the human eyes. This paper studies the effect of small misalignment, specifically a small shift between the input and reference image, on existing metrics, and accordingly develops a shift-tolerant similarity metric. This paper builds upon LPIPS, a widely used learned perceptual similarity metric, and explores architectural design considerations to make it robust against imperceptible misalignment. Specifically, we study a wide spectrum of neural network elements, such as anti-aliasing filtering, pooling, striding, padding, and skip connection, and discuss their roles in making a robust metric. Based on our studies, we develop a new deep neural network-based perceptual similarity metric. Our experiments show that our metric is tolerant to imperceptible shifts while being consistent with the human similarity judgment.
summary: Existing perceptual similarity metrics assume an image and its reference are well aligned. As a result, these metrics are often sensitive to a small alignment error that is imperceptible to the human eyes. This paper studies the effect of small misalignment, specifically a small shift between the input and reference image, on existing metrics, and accordingly develops a shift-tolerant similarity metric. This paper builds upon LPIPS, a widely used learned perceptual similarity metric, and explores architectural design considerations to make it robust against imperceptible misalignment. Specifically, we study a wide spectrum of neural network elements, such as anti-aliasing filtering, pooling, striding, padding, and skip connection, and discuss their roles in making a robust metric. Based on our studies, we develop a new deep neural network-based perceptual similarity metric. Our experiments show that our metric is tolerant to imperceptible shifts while being consistent with the human similarity judgment.
abstract_zh: 现有的感知相似性度量假设图像与其参考完全对齐。因此，这些指标通常对人眼无法察觉的小对齐误差很敏感。本文研究了小偏差（特别是输入图像和参考图像之间的小偏移）对现有度量的影响，并相应地开发了一种可容忍偏移的相似性度量。本文以 LPIPS（一种广泛使用的学习感知相似度度量）为基础，并探讨了架构设计注意事项，以使其对难以察觉的错位具有鲁棒性。具体来说，我们研究了广泛的神经网络元素，例如抗混叠过滤、池化、跨步、填充和跳过连接，并讨论了它们在制定稳健度量中的作用。根据我们的研究，我们开发了一种新的基于深度神经网络的感知相似性度量。我们的实验表明，我们的指标能够容忍难以察觉的变化，同时与人类的相似性判断保持一致。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种对微小位移具有鲁棒性的感知相似性度量方法，基于LPIPS改进以解决对齐误差问题。
AI.motivation: 现有感知相似度量假设图像严格对齐，但实际中微小位移会导致度量敏感而人类难以察觉，需提升位移鲁棒性。
AI.method: 基于LPIPS架构，研究反锯齿、池化、步长、填充和跳跃连接等网络元素对位移鲁棒性的影响，设计新网络结构。
AI.result: 实验表明新度量对不可察觉位移具有容忍性，同时保持与人类相似性判断的一致性。
AI.conclusion: 通过架构改进，提出有效解决微小位移问题的感知相似度量，兼顾鲁棒性与人类感知一致性。

search_text: Shift-tolerant Perceptual Similarity Metric 容错感知相似度度量 Existing perceptual similarity metrics assume an image and its reference are well aligned. As a result, these metrics are often sensitive to a small alignment error that is imperceptible to the human eyes. This paper studies the effect of small misalignment, specifically a small shift between the input and reference image, on existing metrics, and accordingly develops a shift-tolerant similarity metric. This paper builds upon LPIPS, a widely used learned perceptual similarity metric, and explores architectural design considerations to make it robust against imperceptible misalignment. Specifically, we study a wide spectrum of neural network elements, such as anti-aliasing filtering, pooling, striding, padding, and skip connection, and discuss their roles in making a robust metric. Based on our studies, we develop a new deep neural network-based perceptual similarity metric. Our experiments show that our metric is tolerant to imperceptible shifts while being consistent with the human similarity judgment. 现有的感知相似性度量假设图像与其参考完全对齐。因此，这些指标通常对人眼无法察觉的小对齐误差很敏感。本文研究了小偏差（特别是输入图像和参考图像之间的小偏移）对现有度量的影响，并相应地开发了一种可容忍偏移的相似性度量。本文以 LPIPS（一种广泛使用的学习感知相似度度量）为基础，并探讨了架构设计注意事项，以使其对难以察觉的错位具有鲁棒性。具体来说，我们研究了广泛的神经网络元素，例如抗混叠过滤、池化、跨步、填充和跳过连接，并讨论了它们在制定稳健度量中的作用。根据我们的研究，我们开发了一种新的基于深度神经网络的感知相似性度量。我们的实验表明，我们的指标能够容忍难以察觉的变化，同时与人类的相似性判断保持一致。 本文提出一种对微小位移具有鲁棒性的感知相似性度量方法，基于LPIPS改进以解决对齐误差问题。 Abhijay Ghildyal,Feng Liu cs.CV

authors_text: Rowan Border,Jonathan D. Gammell
title: The Surface Edge Explorer (SEE): A measurement-direct approach to next best view planning
title_zh: Surface Edge Explorer (SEE)：用于下一个最佳视图规划的直接测量方法
comment: The International Journal of Robotics Research (IJRR) 2024, Vol. 0(0) 1-27. 25 pages, 17 figures, 6 tables. Videos available at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dqppqRlaGEA and https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLbaQBz4TuPcyNh4COoaCtC1ZGhpbEkFEo
comments: The International Journal of Robotics Research (IJRR) 2024, Vol. 0(0) 1-27. 25 pages, 17 figures, 6 tables. Videos available at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dqppqRlaGEA and https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLbaQBz4TuPcyNh4COoaCtC1ZGhpbEkFEo
abstract: High-quality observations of the real world are crucial for a variety of applications, including producing 3D printed replicas of small-scale scenes and conducting inspections of large-scale infrastructure. These 3D observations are commonly obtained by combining multiple sensor measurements from different views. Guiding the selection of suitable views is known as the NBV planning problem. Most NBV approaches reason about measurements using rigid data structures (e.g., surface meshes or voxel grids). This simplifies next best view selection but can be computationally expensive, reduces real-world fidelity, and couples the selection of a next best view with the final data processing. This paper presents the Surface Edge Explorer, a NBV approach that selects new observations directly from previous sensor measurements without requiring rigid data structures. SEE uses measurement density to propose next best views that increase coverage of insufficiently observed surfaces while avoiding potential occlusions. Statistical results from simulated experiments show that SEE can attain similar or better surface coverage with less observation time and travel distance than evaluated volumetric approaches on both small- and large-scale scenes. Real-world experiments demonstrate SEE autonomously observing a deer statue using a 3D sensor affixed to a robotic arm.
summary: High-quality observations of the real world are crucial for a variety of applications, including producing 3D printed replicas of small-scale scenes and conducting inspections of large-scale infrastructure. These 3D observations are commonly obtained by combining multiple sensor measurements from different views. Guiding the selection of suitable views is known as the NBV planning problem. Most NBV approaches reason about measurements using rigid data structures (e.g., surface meshes or voxel grids). This simplifies next best view selection but can be computationally expensive, reduces real-world fidelity, and couples the selection of a next best view with the final data processing. This paper presents the Surface Edge Explorer, a NBV approach that selects new observations directly from previous sensor measurements without requiring rigid data structures. SEE uses measurement density to propose next best views that increase coverage of insufficiently observed surfaces while avoiding potential occlusions. Statistical results from simulated experiments show that SEE can attain similar or better surface coverage with less observation time and travel distance than evaluated volumetric approaches on both small- and large-scale scenes. Real-world experiments demonstrate SEE autonomously observing a deer statue using a 3D sensor affixed to a robotic arm.
abstract_zh: 对现实世界的高质量观​​察对于各种应用至关重要，包括生产小规模场景的 3D 打印复制品和对大型基础设施进行检查。这些 3D 观测结果通常是通过组合来自不同视图的多个传感器测量值来获得的。指导选择合适的视图称为 NBV 规划问题。大多数 NBV 方法都使用刚性数据结构（例如表面网格或体素网格）来推理测量。这简化了下一个最佳视图的选择，但计算成本可能很高，降低了现实世界的保真度，并将下一个最佳视图的选择与最终的数据处理结合起来。本文介绍了 Surface Edge Explorer，这是一种 NBV 方法，可以直接从以前的传感器测量中选择新的观测值，而不需要严格的数据结构。 SEE 使用测量密度来提出下一个最佳视图，从而增加对观察不足的表面的覆盖范围，同时避免潜在的遮挡。模拟实验的统计结果表明，与评估的小尺度和大尺度场景的体积方法相比，SEE 可以以更少的观测时间和行进距离获得相似或更好的表面覆盖。现实世界的实验表明，SEE 使用固定在机械臂上的 3D 传感器自主观察鹿雕像。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Surface Edge Explorer（SEE）的新型下一最佳视角（NBV）规划方法，该方法直接利用传感器测量数据进行视图选择，无需依赖刚性数据结构。
AI.motivation: 传统NBV方法依赖表面网格或体素网格等刚性数据结构，存在计算成本高、现实世界保真度低以及视图选择与数据处理耦合等问题。
AI.method: SEE通过测量密度直接从历史传感器数据中提出新视角，优先覆盖观察不足的表面区域并避免遮挡，无需构建刚性数据结构。
AI.result: 仿真和真实实验表明，SEE在小规模和大规模场景中均能以更少的观察时间和移动距离实现与现有体素方法相当或更优的表面覆盖率。
AI.conclusion: SEE提供了一种高效、直接的NBV规划框架，在保证观测质量的同时降低了计算复杂度，适用于自主观测系统。

authors_text: Aristotelis Ballas,Christos Diou
title: Multi-layer Representation Learning for Robust OOD Image Classification
title_zh: 用于鲁棒 OOD 图像分类的多层表示学习
comment: Paper accepted for publication in the 12th Hellenic Conference on Artificial Intelligence (SETN 2022)
comments: Paper accepted for publication in the 12th Hellenic Conference on Artificial Intelligence (SETN 2022)
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks have become the norm in image classification. Nevertheless, their difficulty to maintain high accuracy across datasets has become apparent in the past few years. In order to utilize such models in real-world scenarios and applications, they must be able to provide trustworthy predictions on unseen data. In this paper, we argue that extracting features from a CNN's intermediate layers can assist in the model's final prediction. Specifically, we adapt the Hypercolumns method to a ResNet-18 and find a significant increase in the model's accuracy, when evaluating on the NICO dataset.
summary: Convolutional Neural Networks have become the norm in image classification. Nevertheless, their difficulty to maintain high accuracy across datasets has become apparent in the past few years. In order to utilize such models in real-world scenarios and applications, they must be able to provide trustworthy predictions on unseen data. In this paper, we argue that extracting features from a CNN's intermediate layers can assist in the model's final prediction. Specifically, we adapt the Hypercolumns method to a ResNet-18 and find a significant increase in the model's accuracy, when evaluating on the NICO dataset.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络已成为图像分类的标准。然而，在过去几年中，他们在跨数据集上保持高精度的难度已经变得越来越明显。为了在现实场景和应用中利用此类模型，它们必须能够对看不见的数据提供可靠的预测。在本文中，我们认为从 CNN 中间层提取特征可以帮助模型的最终预测。具体来说，我们将 Hypercolumns 方法应用于 ResNet-18，并在 NICO 数据集上进行评估时发现模型的准确性显着提高。

AI.tldr: 本文研究多层表示学习以提升图像分类模型在分布外数据（OOD）上的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 传统CNN在跨数据集场景下准确率下降明显，需提升模型对未见数据的预测可靠性。
AI.method: 将Hypercolumns方法适配至ResNet-18，通过融合中间层特征增强表示学习。
AI.result: 在NICO数据集上模型准确率显著提升，验证了多层特征融合的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 多层特征表示学习能有效增强模型对分布外数据的鲁棒性，为实际应用提供可靠方案。

authors_text: Shixing Yu,Yiyang Ma,Wenhan Yang,Wei Xiang,Jiaying Liu
title: Meta-Interpolation: Time-Arbitrary Frame Interpolation via Dual Meta-Learning
title_zh: 元插值：通过双元学习进行时间任意帧插值
abstract: Existing video frame interpolation methods can only interpolate the frame at a given intermediate time-step, e.g. 1/2. In this paper, we aim to explore a more generalized kind of video frame interpolation, that at an arbitrary time-step. To this end, we consider processing different time-steps with adaptively generated convolutional kernels in a unified way with the help of meta-learning. Specifically, we develop a dual meta-learned frame interpolation framework to synthesize intermediate frames with the guidance of context information and optical flow as well as taking the time-step as side information. First, a content-aware meta-learned flow refinement module is built to improve the accuracy of the optical flow estimation based on the down-sampled version of the input frames. Second, with the refined optical flow and the time-step as the input, a motion-aware meta-learned frame interpolation module generates the convolutional kernels for every pixel used in the convolution operations on the feature map of the coarse warped version of the input frames to generate the predicted frame. Extensive qualitative and quantitative evaluations, as well as ablation studies, demonstrate that, via introducing meta-learning in our framework in such a well-designed way, our method not only achieves superior performance to state-of-the-art frame interpolation approaches but also owns an extended capacity to support the interpolation at an arbitrary time-step.
summary: Existing video frame interpolation methods can only interpolate the frame at a given intermediate time-step, e.g. 1/2. In this paper, we aim to explore a more generalized kind of video frame interpolation, that at an arbitrary time-step. To this end, we consider processing different time-steps with adaptively generated convolutional kernels in a unified way with the help of meta-learning. Specifically, we develop a dual meta-learned frame interpolation framework to synthesize intermediate frames with the guidance of context information and optical flow as well as taking the time-step as side information. First, a content-aware meta-learned flow refinement module is built to improve the accuracy of the optical flow estimation based on the down-sampled version of the input frames. Second, with the refined optical flow and the time-step as the input, a motion-aware meta-learned frame interpolation module generates the convolutional kernels for every pixel used in the convolution operations on the feature map of the coarse warped version of the input frames to generate the predicted frame. Extensive qualitative and quantitative evaluations, as well as ablation studies, demonstrate that, via introducing meta-learning in our framework in such a well-designed way, our method not only achieves superior performance to state-of-the-art frame interpolation approaches but also owns an extended capacity to support the interpolation at an arbitrary time-step.
abstract_zh: 现有的视频帧插值方法只能在给定的中间时间步长处对帧进行插值，例如1/2。在本文中，我们的目标是探索一种更通用的视频帧插值，即任意时间步长的视频帧插值。为此，我们考虑借助元学习以统一的方式使用自适应生成的卷积核来处理不同的时间步。具体来说，我们开发了一个双元学习帧插值框架，以在上下文信息和光流的指导下合成中间帧，并将时间步长作为辅助信息。首先，构建内容感知元学习流细化模块，以提高基于输入帧的下采样版本的光流估计的准确性。其次，以细化的光流和时间步长作为输入，运动感知元学习帧插值模块为输入帧的粗扭曲版本的特征图上的卷积运算中使用的每个像素生成卷积核，以生成预测帧。广泛的定性和定量评估以及消融研究表明，通过以如此精心设计的方式在我们的框架中引入元学习，我们的方法不仅实现了优于最先进的帧插值方法的性能，而且还拥有支持任意时间步插值的扩展能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于双元元学习的视频帧插值方法，能够实现在任意时间步长的帧插值。
AI.motivation: 现有视频帧插值方法仅能处理固定时间步长（如1/2），无法满足任意时间步长的插值需求，限制了应用的灵活性。
AI.method: 通过元学习框架，设计内容感知的光流优化模块和运动感知的帧插值模块，结合上下文信息、光流及时间步长作为侧信息，自适应生成卷积核完成特征图的插值计算。
AI.result: 在大量定性与定量实验中，该方法在任意时间步长插值任务上优于现有最先进方法，且具备更广的适用性。
AI.conclusion: 双元元学习框架通过有效整合上下文与运动信息，成功实现了任意时间步长的高质量帧插值，为视频生成领域提供了新思路。
