search_text: Vector Quantized Image-to-Image Translation 矢量量化图像到图像转换 Current image-to-image translation methods formulate the task with conditional generation models, leading to learning only the recolorization or regional changes as being constrained by the rich structural information provided by the conditional contexts. In this work, we propose introducing the vector quantization technique into the image-to-image translation framework. The vector quantized content representation can facilitate not only the translation, but also the unconditional distribution shared among different domains. Meanwhile, along with the disentangled style representation, the proposed method further enables the capability of image extension with flexibility in both intra- and inter-domains. Qualitative and quantitative experiments demonstrate that our framework achieves comparable performance to the state-of-the-art image-to-image translation and image extension methods. Compared to methods for individual tasks, the proposed method, as a unified framework, unleashes applications combining image-to-image translation, unconditional generation, and image extension altogether. For example, it provides style variability for image generation and extension, and equips image-to-image translation with further extension capabilities. 当前的图像到图像翻译方法使用条件生成模型来制定任务，导致仅学习受条件上下文提供的丰富结构信息约束的重新着色或区域变化。在这项工作中，我们建议将矢量量化技术引入到图像到图像的转换框架中。矢量量化的内容表示不仅可以促进翻译，而且可以促进不同领域之间共享的无条件分布。同时，随着解开的风格表示，所提出的方法进一步实现了在域内和域间灵活的图像扩展能力。定性和定量实验表明，我们的框架实现了与最先进的图像到图像转换和图像扩展方法相当的性能。与单个任务的方法相比，所提出的方法作为一个统一的框架，释放了将图像到图像转换、无条件生成和图像扩展相结合的应用程序。例如，它为图像生成和扩展提供风格可变性，并为图像到图像的翻译提供进一步的扩展功能。 本文提出一种结合向量量化技术的图像到图像翻译框架，以提升跨域翻译与图像扩展能力。 Yu-Jie Chen,Shin-I Cheng,Wei-Chen Chiu,Hung-Yu Tseng,Hsin-Ying Lee cs.CV

authors_text: Dain Kim,Anqi Li,Jonathan Tidor
title: Cubic Goldreich-Levin
title_zh: 立方戈德莱希-莱文
abstract: In this paper, we give a cubic Goldreich-Levin algorithm which makes polynomially-many queries to a function $f \colon \mathbb F_p^n \to \mathbb C$ and produces a decomposition of $f$ as a sum of cubic phases and a small error term. This is a natural higher-order generalization of the classical Goldreich-Levin algorithm. The classical (linear) Goldreich-Levin algorithm has wide-ranging applications in learning theory, coding theory and the construction of pseudorandom generators in cryptography, as well as being closely related to Fourier analysis. Higher-order Goldreich-Levin algorithms on the other hand involve central problems in higher-order Fourier analysis, namely the inverse theory of the Gowers $U^k$ norms, which are well-studied in additive combinatorics. The only known result in this direction prior to this work is the quadratic Goldreich-Levin theorem, proved by Tulsiani and Wolf in 2011. The main step of their result involves an algorithmic version of the $U^3$ inverse theorem. More complications appear in the inverse theory of the $U^4$ and higher norms. Our cubic Goldreich-Levin algorithm is based on algorithmizing recent work by Gowers and Milićević who proved new quantitative bounds for the $U^4$ inverse theorem. Our cubic Goldreich-Levin algorithm is constructed from two main tools: an algorithmic $U^4$ inverse theorem and an arithmetic decomposition result in the style of the Frieze-Kannan graph regularity lemma. As one application of our main theorem we solve the problem of self-correction for cubic Reed-Muller codes beyond the list decoding radius. Additionally we give a purely combinatorial result: an improvement of the quantitative bounds on the $U^4$ inverse theorem.

Higher-order Goldreich-Levin algorithms on the other hand involve central problems in higher-order Fourier analysis, namely the inverse theory of the Gowers $U^k$ norms, which are well-studied in additive combinatorics. The only known result in this direction prior to this work is the quadratic Goldreich-Levin theorem, proved by Tulsiani and Wolf in 2011. The main step of their result involves an algorithmic version of the $U^3$ inverse theorem. $U^4$ 和更高范数的逆理论出现了更多的复杂性。 Our cubic Goldreich-Levin algorithm is based on algorithmizing recent work by Gowers and Milićević who proved new quantitative bounds for the $U^4$ inverse theorem. Our cubic Goldreich-Levin algorithm is constructed from two main tools: an algorithmic $U^4$ inverse theorem and an arithmetic decomposition result in the style of the Frieze-Kannan graph regularity lemma.作为我们的主要定理的一种应用，我们解决了超出列表解码半径的立方里德穆勒码的自校正问题。此外，我们给出了一个纯粹的组合结果：$U^4$ 逆定理的定量界限的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种三次Goldreich-Levin算法，通过多项式次数的查询将函数分解为三次相位和小误差项。
AI.motivation: 经典Goldreich-Levin算法在学习理论、编码理论和密码学中应用广泛，但高阶版本在更高阶傅里叶分析中面临挑战，尤其涉及Gowers范数逆定理的算法化问题。
AI.method: 基于Gowers和Milićević的U⁴逆定理量化界算法化，结合类似Frieze-Kannan图正则化引理的算术分解方法。
AI.result: 实现了三次Goldreich-Levin算法，解决了三次Reed-Muller码的自校正问题，并改进了U⁴逆定理的量化界。
AI.conclusion: 该算法推动了高阶傅里叶分析的发展，为编码理论和组合数学提供了新工具。

authors_text: Aditi Partap,Samuel Grayson,Muhammad Huzaifa,Sarita Adve,Brighten Godfrey,Saurabh Gupta,Kris Hauser,Radhika Mittal
title: On-Device CPU Scheduling for Sense-React Systems
title_zh: Sense-React 系统的设备内 CPU 调度
comment: 13 pages, 13 figures. This version of the paper extends a shorter version that has been accepted at IROS'22
comments: 13 pages, 13 figures. This version of the paper extends a shorter version that has been accepted at IROS'22
abstract: Sense-react systems (e.g. robotics and AR/VR) have to take highly responsive real-time actions, driven by complex decisions involving a pipeline of sensing, perception, planning, and reaction tasks. These tasks must be scheduled on resource-constrained devices such that the performance goals and the requirements of the application are met. This is a difficult scheduling problem that requires handling multiple scheduling dimensions, and variations in resource usage and availability. In practice, system designers manually tune parameters for their specific hardware and application, which results in poor generalization and increases the development burden. In this work, we highlight the emerging need for scheduling CPU resources at runtime in sense-react systems. We study three canonical applications (face tracking, robot navigation, and VR) to first understand the key scheduling requirements for such systems. Armed with this understanding, we develop a scheduling framework, Catan, that dynamically schedules compute resources across different components of an app so as to meet the specified application requirements. Through experiments with a prototype implemented on a widely-used robotics framework (ROS) and an open-source AR/VR platform, we show the impact of system scheduling on meeting the performance goals for the three applications, how Catan is able to achieve better application performance than hand-tuned configurations, and how it dynamically adapts to runtime variations.
summary: Sense-react systems (e.g. robotics and AR/VR) have to take highly responsive real-time actions, driven by complex decisions involving a pipeline of sensing, perception, planning, and reaction tasks. These tasks must be scheduled on resource-constrained devices such that the performance goals and the requirements of the application are met. This is a difficult scheduling problem that requires handling multiple scheduling dimensions, and variations in resource usage and availability. In practice, system designers manually tune parameters for their specific hardware and application, which results in poor generalization and increases the development burden. In this work, we highlight the emerging need for scheduling CPU resources at runtime in sense-react systems. We study three canonical applications (face tracking, robot navigation, and VR) to first understand the key scheduling requirements for such systems. Armed with this understanding, we develop a scheduling framework, Catan, that dynamically schedules compute resources across different components of an app so as to meet the specified application requirements. Through experiments with a prototype implemented on a widely-used robotics framework (ROS) and an open-source AR/VR platform, we show the impact of system scheduling on meeting the performance goals for the three applications, how Catan is able to achieve better application performance than hand-tuned configurations, and how it dynamically adapts to runtime variations.
abstract_zh: 感知-反应系统（例如机器人和 AR/VR）必须采取高度响应的实时行动，由涉及传感、感知、规划和反应任务管道的复杂决策驱动。这些任务必须安排在资源受限的设备上，以便满足应用程序的性能目标和要求。这是一个困难的调度问题，需要处理多个调度维度以及资源使用和可用性的变化。在实践中，系统设计人员针对其特定硬件和应用手动调整参数，这会导致通用性较差并增加开发负担。在这项工作中，我们强调了在 sense-react 系统中在运行时调度 CPU 资源的新兴需求。我们研究了三种典型应用（面部跟踪、机器人导航和 VR），首先了解此类系统的关键调度要求。有了这种理解，我们开发了一个调度框架 Catan，它可以跨应用程序的不同组件动态调度计算资源，以满足指定的应用程序需求。通过在广泛使用的机器人框架 (ROS) 和开源 AR/VR 平台上实现的原型进行实验，我们展示了系统调度对满足三个应用程序性能目标的影响、Catan 如何能够实现比手动调整配置更好的应用程序性能，以及它如何动态适应运行时变化。

AI.tldr: 本文针对感知-反应系统（如机器人、AR/VR）在资源受限设备上的实时任务调度问题，提出了一种动态CPU资源分配框架Catan。
AI.motivation: 现有系统依赖人工调参，导致性能目标难以满足且缺乏通用性，需在运行时动态调度CPU资源以适应实时任务需求。
AI.method: 通过分析三个典型应用（人脸跟踪、机器人导航、VR）的调度需求，设计动态资源分配框架Catan，并在ROS和开源AR/VR平台原型上验证。
AI.result: 实验表明Catan在满足性能目标方面优于人工配置，且能动态适应运行时资源变化，提升应用整体表现。
AI.conclusion: 动态调度框架可有效解决感知-反应系统的资源分配难题，为实时应用提供通用化、自动化的调度方案。

search_text: On-Device CPU Scheduling for Sense-React Systems Sense-React 系统的设备内 CPU 调度 Sense-react systems (e.g. robotics and AR/VR) have to take highly responsive real-time actions, driven by complex decisions involving a pipeline of sensing, perception, planning, and reaction tasks. These tasks must be scheduled on resource-constrained devices such that the performance goals and the requirements of the application are met. This is a difficult scheduling problem that requires handling multiple scheduling dimensions, and variations in resource usage and availability. In practice, system designers manually tune parameters for their specific hardware and application, which results in poor generalization and increases the development burden. In this work, we highlight the emerging need for scheduling CPU resources at runtime in sense-react systems. We study three canonical applications (face tracking, robot navigation, and VR) to first understand the key scheduling requirements for such systems. Armed with this understanding, we develop a scheduling framework, Catan, that dynamically schedules compute resources across different components of an app so as to meet the specified application requirements. Through experiments with a prototype implemented on a widely-used robotics framework (ROS) and an open-source AR/VR platform, we show the impact of system scheduling on meeting the performance goals for the three applications, how Catan is able to achieve better application performance than hand-tuned configurations, and how it dynamically adapts to runtime variations. 感知-反应系统（例如机器人和 AR/VR）必须采取高度响应的实时行动，由涉及传感、感知、规划和反应任务管道的复杂决策驱动。这些任务必须安排在资源受限的设备上，以便满足应用程序的性能目标和要求。这是一个困难的调度问题，需要处理多个调度维度以及资源使用和可用性的变化。在实践中，系统设计人员针对其特定硬件和应用手动调整参数，这会导致通用性较差并增加开发负担。在这项工作中，我们强调了在 sense-react 系统中在运行时调度 CPU 资源的新兴需求。我们研究了三种典型应用（面部跟踪、机器人导航和 VR），首先了解此类系统的关键调度要求。有了这种理解，我们开发了一个调度框架 Catan，它可以跨应用程序的不同组件动态调度计算资源，以满足指定的应用程序需求。通过在广泛使用的机器人框架 (ROS) 和开源 AR/VR 平台上实现的原型进行实验，我们展示了系统调度对满足三个应用程序性能目标的影响、Catan 如何能够实现比手动调整配置更好的应用程序性能，以及它如何动态适应运行时变化。 本文针对感知-反应系统（如机器人、AR/VR）在资源受限设备上的实时任务调度问题，提出了一种动态CPU资源分配框架Catan。 Aditi Partap,Samuel Grayson,Muhammad Huzaifa,Sarita Adve,Brighten Godfrey,Saurabh Gupta,Kris Hauser,Radhika Mittal cs.RO
